programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: Ellen Mallam is left to drown in an upturned car by its driver, but the young woman's connection to James Blackstone, an aristocratic family's heir apparent, impedes Gently and Bacchus's investigation. The chief inspector discovers that the son's future has been carefully mapped out since birth by his implacable mother Alethea, and she will not countenance any interference in her plans. However, an astonishing twist causes the detectives to reconsider the case in its entirety. Sixties-set detective drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Geraldine Somerville, James Norton, Roger Lloyd Pack, Christopher Fairbank and Ebony Buckle.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An elderly amateur sleuth who was a friend of Danny's is murdered, and he ends up embroiled in the case when his photo is found at the scene. However, the cold case copper becomes convinced that the secret to uncovering her real killer lies within the last case she was working on. Picking up where she left off, he travels to the village of Minchampton, where a woman was said to have been murdered by a local lad 25 years previously. Meanwhile, Sasha is left to cope with the investigating officer on the modern-day Miss Marple's murder - an overbearing contemporary from her Hendon training, by the name of DCI Grace Mackie - and Steve lets Gerry stay with him when he is evicted, but soon comes to regret the act of kindness.(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Leo and Harry's professional rivalry erupts following Sam's departure. They deal with the case of several children who experienced unexplained breathing difficulties, leaving one of them dead. While Harry believes this was mass hysteria, Leo suspects something more sinister. Meanwhile, a man is found dead in his car with a stab wound to the chest. Tom Ward and William Gaminara star.(n)

programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.desc: A look inside a bingo hall in the heart of West Belfast, meeting some of the regulars who have known each other for many years, through thick and thin. The programme follows bingo members including serenely composed Eilish, who was taught how to play by her grandmother when she was in her teens, and hard-working Margaret, who juggles her bingo alongside work, a college course, school pick-ups and supporting her husband as he deals with health problems.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

programme.desc: Comedy in which actors David Tennant and Michael Sheen play themselves as they prepare for a stage production while under lockdown. The Good Omens co-stars were due to put on a play in the West End before the production was put on hold because of the coronavirus pandemic. Not wanting to let his big break pass him by, director Simon Evans must do his best to convince his doubtful actors to carry on rehearsals online.(n)

programme.title: Staged
programme.desc: With rehearsals due to begin, director Simon goes missing. Michael and David embrace the opportunity to skive off from their duties, but one of them gets caught breaking the law. Georgia is due to deliver her novel to her agent and asks David to stand in and help with the kids.(n)

programme.title: The Mysterious Murder of Nipsey Hussle
programme.desc: Ben Zand sets out to unravel the mystery of rapper and entrepreneur Nipsey Hussle's murder, which took place in March 2019, travelling to Los Angeles to meet those closest to him.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Joe Swift shows how to bring a lawn back to life, AJ Rose demonstrates how easy it is to put down decking, and Jay has a tip to help keep tools tidy.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift celebrate the positive power of plants as they focus on the designers and growers striving for a better world with the creations they bring to the event. They reveal how, over the years, the show has contributed to the better promotion of the health of the planet, plants and people, and talk to Countryfile presenter Ellie Harrison and BBC correspondent Fergal Keane about the importance of gardens to mental wellbeing.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Iona in the Inner Hebrides to learn about St Columba's tough brand of monasticism, and finds out how the epic journey of St Cuthbert led to the writing of the Lindisfarne Gospels and the building of Durham Cathedral. At Canterbury Cathedral, Neil discovers how the gruesome murder of Thomas Becket spurred the building of its nave, and on Glastonbury Tor in Somerset, he investigates the story of the Holy Grail, the sacred cup of everlasting life.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Dan Walker hosts the strategic game show in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen go on a culinary journey around the nation, following a route mapped out by the adventurous gourmand Samuel Chamberlain in his 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. They begin in the Dorset seaside town of Weymouth, where they sample lots of fresh seafood before heading to the countryside to eat Portland lamb.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Yugoslavia v Spain. A chance to see the Euro 2000 Group C encounter at Jan Breydel Stadium in Bruges, Belgium.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and James Lewis value antiques and collectibles at St Albans Cathedral and Abbey in Hertfordshire, while Paul Martin visits nearby Knebworth House.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit dramatic events at Longleat. Kate visits the red panda maternity suite, Alfie the gorilla has surgery to address a recurring infection on his jaw, and the park goes into lockdown after reports of an escaped animal. Back in Animal Adventure, Jean Johansson checks in with keeper Kim and Orville the corn snake.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family give up their 21st century technology and travel back in time to discover the radical transformation of our leisure time since 1950. Guided by presenter Giles Coren and social historian Polly Russell, the family's experience is underpinned by The Family Expenditure Survey, a government study which ran from the 1950s through to 1999. In the first episode, dad Rob finds out that he's expected to be handy with a tool kit, while mum Steph comes to terms with all the washing, cooking and cleaning expected of a 1950s housewife. Meanwhile, 16-year-old Daisy learns ballroom dancing courtesy of Angela Rippon, and 12-year-old Seth discovers the great outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Walkers Crisps factory in Leicester, which he visited in 2016, finding out how they are coping during the coronavirus crisis. He speaks to manager Carl Lewis, who reveals that in order to keep up with demand they're making big changes to their output, switching away from single packets to a situation where 90 per cent of production is now the multipacks sold in supermarkets.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Vic Reeves does his inimitable impersonation of Graham Norton, Sara Pascoe teaches yoga with her rescue dog, Reginald D Hunter gives his unique insight into conspiracy theories and Matt Berry showcases his exclusive anti-virus song. Also featuring Bob Mortimer, Tony Way, Adam Buxton and some very relaxed cats.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as James Acaster, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Darren Harriott and Zoe Lyons join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look at the news. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the house's fortunes in the 18th and 19th centuries. He begins by investigating the death of owner Joseph Holbrook, and discovering why his daughter was written out of the will. A later resident of the home rises to become mayor in the 1830s, but had to find a way to prevent riots that had ravaged the city years earlier from occurring again. David also delves into the life of a servant who worked in the house in the 1870s, only to leave domestic servitude for an abusive marriage, and discovers how a successful teacher who later owned the house ended up committed to an asylum.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven is at home in his garden, where he gives tips on what butterflies to look out for and how to get involved in a nationwide survey. He also catches up with NHS fundraiser Tom Moore to discover what role the countryside has played in his life. Shepherdess Hannah Jackson puts her two new collie pups through their first paces as working dogs, and Adam Henson turns teacher as he takes his piglets to the country's home-schooled children.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A boy taking part in a karting race is sent flying into the air as his father watches from the stands, and a man who dived into a river to save a dog needs rescuing as well.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don, Joe Swift and the team look at the legends and legacies that have made the Chelsea Flower Show the greatest event in the horticultural calendar. They discover what it takes to become an icon at this show, talk to the Chelsea designers and plants men and women that have broken records, won global recognition and inspired generations of gardeners. Plus, how movers and shakers from other creative fields have been influenced by their visits to the event over the years.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: Documentary following a mentorship scheme designed to help new nurses stay in the profession and reverse a trend in which more than a third are looking for new roles. Newly qualified nurse Lauren has reached breaking point in one of the busiest A&E departments in the UK, so mentor Taz tries to negotiates an urgent move for her to a new part of the hospital.(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Raj Bisram help the teams look for items at Ardingly International Antiques and Collectors Fair in West Sussex, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visits a windmill that has been turned into a museum of rural life.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms - and the memories they hold - back to life, tackling a 1960s cine-projector, a wicker chair and a hand-crafted spinning wheel that has travelled all the way from Shetland.(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part one of two. The broadcaster joins a team of historians and geologists to recreate a pioneering 280-mile journey through the natural wonder that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Following in Powell's footsteps in the stunning yet challenging north Arizona landscape, Snow and the crew take on the area's rapids in antique wooden boats as they learn about the region's geological and Native American past.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Strategic game show hosted by Dan Walker, in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: In the ancient market town of Devizes, Terry Wogan and cabbie Mason McQueen are on the hunt for the finest flavours in town. They sample local foods including the famous Wiltshire cured bacon, and navigating the famous Calne locks helps them appreciate a home-made bread and butter pudding.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Ukraine v Northern Ireland. Another chance to see the Euro 2016 Group C encounter from Parc Olympique Lyonnais in France.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Lewis are at Herstmonceux Castle in East Sussex, where a Chinese wall plaque catches James's eye, and Paul Martin explores the history of the building.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Keepers battle to get the rhinos to take a skin soothing mud bath, and the vets must get to the root of Arabella the South East Asian binturong's anger issues.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family sample life in the 1960s and with more money to go around, there's more fun to be had, including a trip to the seaside, a Dansette record player for 16-year-old Daisy and even their own Mini to enjoy. Dad Rob and son Seth take on football legend Trevor Brooking for a game of Subbuteo in the dining room, and Daisy and mum Steph meet Sandie Shaw to discover how the decade's daring fashions reflected the growing freedoms and confidence enjoyed by the younger generation.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Iolo Williams and Gillian Burke present the latest updates from the cameras set up across the country, including a portrait of the Lake District. Ecologist Ellie Harrison shares wildlife stories live from her home in the Cotswolds, and nature cameraman Raymond Besant encounters otters in the Orkney Islands.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga explores the house's history from the 1880s to the First World War, a period that saw the property sliding down the social scale and turning from a family home to a multiple occupancy. He discovers how a resident responded to the death of his baby daughter by opening a milk bar for the temperance movement, only to get into legal trouble for watering down his product. David also discovers the story behind a mysterious occupant who lived in the house under a pseudonym and learns how the battle of Passchendaele changed the residents' lives forever.(n)

programme.title: I'll Get This - Extra Helping
programme.desc: Tom Davis, Julian Clary, Laura Whitmore, Rachel Johnson and Chris Kamara meet at a restaurant for a fancy meal. The catch is they'll play games between courses and the loser will have to pay for everyone's dinner.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: House of Saud: A Family at War
programme.desc: Documentary following Saudi Arabia's Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, who has pledged to transform the country. Cameras examine the new leader's dramatic commitment to end extremism and to return to moderate Islam. Travelling from Eastern Europe to India, and across the bloody battlefields of Syria and Yemen, it traces the impact that huge amounts of Saudi cash and weapons have had around the world.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the eight remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room for lingerie and sleepwear week. To test the sewers' handling skills, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a boned basque. The task requires sewing together small pattern pieces for a precise fit and creating a tricky hook and eye fastening. Next, in the transformation challenge, the sewers transform pyjamas and nightwear into a summer dress, before finally being tasked to create a two-piece female sleep set which has to incorporates lace somewhere in their design.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: George Anderson hankers after hostas, Kirsty Wilson harks back to the '70s to create an easy terrarium and Chris Beardshaw deals with a very bad bout of Box Blight.(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: Two divers documenting a wreck in the Thames find more than they bargained for and call in the Royal Navy bomb team. An unsafe load on a vehicle flies off, landing on a windscreen.(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Saving Our Nurses
programme.desc: A new midwife questions whether she is cut out for her vocation when she fails to master a vital technique - but her mentor has a plan to help her perfect her skills.(n)

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Visitors try to get a good deal on an unusual key, a harmonica, an early 20th-century underwear advert, and a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison create virtual reality renovations for a couple's home in Margate. The property was originally a B&B, and the transition to a home has not been entirely successful. It is poorly laid out, has too many rooms, and while big on floor space is lacking natural light. Laura sets out to return the building to its early 20th-century roots, whole Robert comes up with a radically different way of using the living space.(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen embark on the third leg of the tour around Britain, following Samuel Chamberlain's 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. Arriving in York, they sample one of the city's signature hams, and the confusingly named savoury duck, before heading to Ampleforth for more culinary treats. Finally, their visit concludes with a riotous Viking feast, hosted by Eric the Red.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Sweden v England. A chance to see the Euro 2012 Group D encounter at the Olympic Stadium in Kiev.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman has his first taste of Korean barbecue food and samples Swedish cuisine, and Ainsley Harriott cooks a salmon and mango salad inspired by the day's adventures.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents previously unseen finds from the show's travels round the country, visiting Muncaster Castle, the Bowes Museum and Norwich Cathedral. Antiques experts include Kate Bateman, Paul Laidlaw, Jonathan Pratt, James Lewis and Thomas Plant, with items ranging from a pair of Lalique vases to a 9.5mm projector.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Vets must try and repair the hands of Maurice and Angelo, two injured ring-tail lemurs. Elsewhere, the keepers are on standby as a seahorse gives birth.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family strut into the 1970s and some discover there were unexpected upsides to the economic and political turmoil the decade is often remembered for. Rob and Steph share more time together, playing darts with Eric Bristow and taking on some period appropriate home improvement. Meanwhile, daughter Daisy lets her hair down at a roller disco with DJ Trevor Nelson, and the family receive a visit from Top Gear's original presenter, Angela Rippon.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Gillian Burke, Iolo Williams and Gordon Buchanan share more seasonal stories from Spring 2020. Iolo shares his own personal stories about the wildflowers growing in his local hedgerows and cameraman Sam Oakes documents the fortunes of two very different long tailed tit nests.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: When a new victim is found, it becomes increasingly clear that the murderer's main targets are not the victims themselves, but their loved ones. Police work to establish a link between the two deaths, but crucial information continues to elude them. Meanwhile, John and Liz grow closer as they focus on the case. Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author Giles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell, Karine Vanasse, Glen Gould and Kristen Thomson.(n)

programme.title: Cardinal
programme.desc: Another abduction hits painfully close to home for Lise, as she and John race against time to save the next victim before it is too late. Crime thriller, based on the John Cardinal Mysteries series by Canadian author GIles Blunt, starring Billy Campbell, Karine Vanasse and Glen Gould.(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton visits some of the most beautiful and remote spots in Britain, meeting the people who live there and discovering more about Scotland's best-kept secrets. In this episode, he visits the low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: House of Saud: A Family at War
programme.desc: How some of the most prominent and richest Saudi royals were caught up in an unprecedented crackdown on corruption. Hundreds of the richest people in the Kingdom, including 11 members of the Royal Family, were kept in a luxurious five-star hotel. The detentions opened the door on a highly secret world of kickbacks and bribes on a monumental scale.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross create a peaceful, dewy-brown mountain scene featuring a fresh morning lake and succulent evergreens.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: An insight into the creative processes and cultural significance of traditional artefacts from the Pacific region, beginning with making of an Aboriginal didgeridoo.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines the post-Thatcher years, considering the nation's final shift from an imperial power to an island at the heart of a strong world economy. He also recalls noteworthy developments under the leadership of John Major and Tony Blair, including Black Wednesday and the increasing awareness of global warming.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Autopsy: The Secret Story of Our Seas
programme.desc: Oceanographer Helen Czerski and zoologist George McGavin examine the health of the North Sea's ecosytem to reveal the startling changes the world's oceans are undergoing. They investigate the effects of high levels of life-threatening toxins on marine life, and the invisible plague of micro and nano plastics saturating the water, as well as a species of fungi that can potentially consume these microplastics. George also visits Utrecht University to see leading animal pathologist Lonneke Ijsseldijk perform a necropsy on a harbour porpoise to try to find out how and why it died.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby tells the story of the power struggle between the monarchy and the Church during the Middle Ages through treasures of the period, and reveals how the code of chivalry brought the two sides together. He also reassesses the cultural impact of Richard II's reign, and examines artefacts including the Mappa Mundi, Chaucer's Canterbury Tales and Britain's only preserved medieval crown.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Renaissance
programme.desc: Art historian Dr James Fox celebrates an era from the early 1500s to the mid-1600s that saw Britain shed its medieval shackles and embrace a world of cutting-edge art, literature, architecture and science. In the opening edition, he explores how the Renaissance arrived on the nation's shores when a handful of influential European artists brought ideas from the continent in the early 16th century, sparking a cultural revolution.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross create a peaceful, dewy-brown mountain scene featuring a fresh morning lake and succulent evergreens.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: An insight into the creative processes and cultural significance of traditional artefacts from the Pacific region, beginning with making of an Aboriginal didgeridoo.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Autopsy: The Secret Story of Our Seas
programme.desc: Oceanographer Helen Czerski and zoologist George McGavin examine the health of the North Sea's ecosytem to reveal the startling changes the world's oceans are undergoing. They investigate the effects of high levels of life-threatening toxins on marine life, and the invisible plague of micro and nano plastics saturating the water, as well as a species of fungi that can potentially consume these microplastics. George also visits Utrecht University to see leading animal pathologist Lonneke Ijsseldijk perform a necropsy on a harbour porpoise to try to find out how and why it died.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a narrow and winding creek flowing slowly through a rocky meadow forest.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: One of the last traditional weavers on the Polynesian island of Rurutu creates a ceremonial taupoo - an ornate hat worn on special occasions - from dried pandanus leaves. Taking around five weeks to make, these hats were originally introduced to the island by British missionaries in the early 1800s, and now are worn to church and given as wedding gifts.(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.sub-title: The Hidden Killers of the Tudor Home
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly.(n)

programme.title: Deep Ocean
programme.sub-title: Deep Ocean: Giants of the Antarctic Deep
programme.desc: Documentary about the first scientific research undertaken in a submersible in the Antarctic. Three marine biologists use customised UHD cameras to capture ling the extraordinary forms of life that thrive in the Southern Ocean on film. Narrated by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.title: Deep Ocean
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following a crew attempting to film bioluminescent creatures in a huge underwater canyon in California's Monterey Bay.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: The documentary explores the intelligence of marine mammals, discovering just how smart whales and dolphins really are. Scientist Denise Herzing talks about the development of a two-way communication system between dolphins and humans in the Bahamas, while in Western Australia the creatures are filmed using a range of survival skills. In Mexico, grey whales seemingly empathise with humans, while the humpbacks of Alaska pool their talents to increase hunting success. Narrated by Stephen Fry.(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook concludes his exploration of the genre by considering the allure of time travel, with the power to change the past or see the future being a theme that writers and film-makers have embraced for many years. From HG Wells' pioneering scientist in The Time Machine, to Doc and Marty McFly in Back to the Future, audiences have been presented with a host of colourful travellers and their machines. Featuring contributions by former Doctor Who stars David Tennant and Karen Gillan as well as current showrunner Steven Moffat, actor Christopher Lloyd and screenwriter Bob Gale (Back to the Future), Quantum Leap star Scott Bakula and The Time Traveler's Wife author Audrey Niffenegger.(n)

programme.title: Looking for Rembrandt
programme.desc: The story behind Rembrandt's most iconic work, The Night Watch. The events that surrounded the creation of his masterpiece contributed to a 10-year decline in his output, as he devoted his energies to a series of etchings inspired by his extra-marital affairs, while a struggle to manage his debts led him to declare bankruptcy.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a picture of a narrow and winding creek flowing slowly through a rocky meadow forest.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: One of the last traditional weavers on the Polynesian island of Rurutu creates a ceremonial taupoo - an ornate hat worn on special occasions - from dried pandanus leaves. Taking around five weeks to make, these hats were originally introduced to the island by British missionaries in the early 1800s, and now are worn to church and given as wedding gifts.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines the post-Thatcher years, considering the nation's final shift from an imperial power to an island at the heart of a strong world economy. He also recalls noteworthy developments under the leadership of John Major and Tony Blair, including Black Wednesday and the increasing awareness of global warming.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles and dark fresh air.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: With chainsaws, adzes and more than 30 different chisels, master Maori carver Logan Okiwi Shipgood crafts a beautiful 6ft tall 'pou' statue from native New Zealand timber.(n)

programme.title: Shock and Awe: The Story of Electricity
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili charts how scientists discovered the connection between electricity and magnetism, and explains why this breakthrough gave mankind far greater control over electrical forces. He also reveals that their findings were crucial to the development of electricity as a mass-produced source of power, leading to innovations in communications, manufacturing, transport and technology.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre examines how continued success for the unit could only be achieved with ever more dangerous and ambitious missions, while David Stirling's new second-in-command Captain Paddy Mayne was an officer as unpredictable and dangerous as the phase of war that was about to begin. The historian also reveals how many SAS members were captured by the Germans - and hears the extraordinary story of two of them who outran their pursuers by surviving for three days in the desert without food or fresh water.(n)

programme.title: The Making of Spain
programme.sub-title: Blood and Gold: The Making of Spain with Simon Sebag Montefiore
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore explores the history of Spain, beginning with the country's early years, when Iberia was a minor province of Carthage - then the most coveted of Rome's colonies - through to Spain's Muslim age and the Cordoba Caliphate.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates this documentary exploring the vocal ranges of marine mammals. Sperm whales produce clicks to locate giant squid two miles beneath the ocean surface, and narwhals use a similar technique to find breathing holes in Arctic ice floes. Bottlenose dolphins communicate with fishermen in Brazil, humpbacks offer haunting operatic performances and boto dolphins create bat-like ultrasonic calls to help find fish in the murky Amazon River.(n)

programme.title: Sitting In Limbo
programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.desc: Up-and-coming writer Arabella returns from visiting her boyfriend in Italy and is greeted by a deluge of frantic messages from her literary agents. With a matter of hours left to finish a draft of her second book, she plans to pull an all-nighter - but is soon tempted to join a friend on a night out. programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.sub-title: Are You Leading or Am I?
programme.desc: Konstantin makes a break for it, while Eve and Villanelle try to work out what the future may hold for them - together or apart. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer, Sandra Oh and Kim Bodnia.(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Big Night at the Bingo - Our Lives
programme.desc: A look inside a bingo hall in the heart of West Belfast, meeting some of the regulars who have known each other for many years, through thick and thin. The programme follows bingo members including serenely composed Eilish, who was taught how to play by her grandmother when she was in her teens, and hard-working Margaret, who juggles her bingo alongside work, a college course, school pick-ups and supporting her husband as he deals with health problems.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian arranges a memorial for Dennis and Sharon is touched by the effort he has put in. Gray finds Whitney disorientated on the street and takes her back to his place, where they go over the night Leo was killed. Mick and Linda have their eyes on a house to rent, but they are worried about Phil paying them with dodgy money for The Vic. Peter asks Dotty to fix his car but she refuses.(n)

programme.title: Sitting In Limbo
programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Up-and-coming writer Arabella returns from visiting her boyfriend in Italy and is greeted by a deluge of frantic messages from her literary agents. With a matter of hours left to finish a draft of her second book, she plans to pull an all-nighter - but is soon tempted to join a friend on a night out. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Paapa Essiedu.(n)

programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Rebecca pays a visit to Joe's school to teach the class about pregnancy, an experience which eases her worries about her own child's future. Ben returns from work in Derby with bad news for Alison. Ralph moves into his new flat with Katie, and Louise finds the experience of her son leaving home hard to take - but Maurice proposes a drastic solution to her loneliness.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Essie attempts to act as if there is nothing to worry about as she starts chemotherapy, but confides in Fletch that she knows they will both face difficult decisions in the future. Guy's past is dredged up, but gets the chance to prove he is better than his reputation - if he can convince Ric to let him operate. Jac is determined to secure a bright future for Darwin after losing a profitable contract, but fears her feelings for Kian may be a distraction.(n)

programme.title: The A Word
programme.desc: Rebecca pays a visit to Joe's school to teach the class about pregnancy, an experience which eases her worries about her own child's future. However, while driving back to the Lakes, she goes into labour, and faces the prospect of giving birth by the roadside with only Joe on hand to help her. Ben returns from work in Derby with bad news for Alison. Ralph moves into his new flat with Katie, and Louise finds the experience of her son leaving home hard to take - but Maurice proposes a drastic solution to her loneliness.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.sub-title: Are You Leading or Am I?
programme.desc: Konstantin makes a break for it, while Eve and Villanelle try to work out what the future may hold for them - together or apart. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer, Sandra Oh and Kim Bodnia.(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Big Night at the Bingo - Our Lives
programme.desc: A look inside a bingo hall in the heart of West Belfast, meeting some of the regulars who have known each other for many years, through thick and thin. The programme follows bingo members including serenely composed Eilish, who was taught how to play by her grandmother when she was in her teens, and hard-working Margaret, who juggles her bingo alongside work, a college course, school pick-ups and supporting her husband as he deals with health problems.(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the five remaining contestants take part in the quarter final, creating garments and trying their hand at techniques from around the world. Firstly, the sewers must follow a pattern like they have never encountered before, as judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young ask them to make a Filipino Terno Blouse. In the Transformation Challenge, the competitors are asked to turn Provencal tablecloths from France into a brand new outfit in just 90 minutes, before creating a dramatic Flamenco skirt from Southern Spain in the final challenge.(n)

The Good Omens co-stars were due to put on a play in the West End before the production was put on hold because of the coronavirus pandemic. Not wanting to let his big break pass him by, director Simon Evans must do his best to convince his doubtful actors to carry on rehearsals online.(n)

programme.title: Staged

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: The duo investigate heavy and light.(n)

programme.title: Piseag & Cuilean/Kit & Pup
programme.desc: Pup and Kit examine inside and outside.(n)

programme.title: Bruno
programme.desc: The playful monkey wiggles like a worm.(n)

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte
programme.sub-title: Pàdraig Post agus An Uèir-slaighdidh Shiùbhlach (Postman Pat and the Zooming Zip)
programme.desc: The long-serving mailman faces a long and rocky path when attempting to deliver a hefty parcel.(n)

programme.title: Baile Mhuilinn
programme.desc: Gaelic songs and stories for children learning to read, featuring the letter U.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Calum Maclean reads Beag air Bheag by Amber Stewart, illustrated by Layn Marlow and translated into Gaelic by Donald John Macleod.(n)

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Eòseph has cerebral palsy, but is determined to walk to his own birthday party.(n)

programme.title: Buntàta Amh (Big Tatties)
programme.desc: The potato tries to adhere to a diet from the Second World War.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to make musical instruments and a battery-powered burglar alarm. Presented by Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.sub-title: Roilidh an t-Sròn-0adharcach Dubh (Roly the Black Rhino)
programme.desc: Documentary following the story of Roly, a baby black rhino growing up on the African plains who has a lot to learn in its first year, from wallowing in mud to browsing. The programme explores what frightens rhinos, why a certain little bird is their best friend, and how much Roly will have to eat each day to grow and become one of the largest animals on earth.(n)

programme.title: Sugh an Eorna (History of Whisky)
programme.desc: John Carmichael presents a six-part series celebrating whisky through the centuries, beginning with a visit to the distilleries in the Borders and the cities of the Lowlands, and a sample of the region's best-known malts.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: A look at music trends, including electronic and brass.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Fuine (Home Baking)
programme.desc: Gregor Macleod is in Benbecula to taste crisp puff pastry pasties filled with the freshest Uist crab meat and melt-in-the-mouth leeks. In Beauly he meets a woman who uses amaretti biscuits paired with raspberries to add texture and flavour to a pudding.(n)

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald investigates the work of some of the Gaelic poets who have influenced her over the years.(n)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Alasdair excuses himself to the city without telling Màiri the real reason for his visit, only to come home and find Màiri and Donneil together - and close. Little does Iain know that he's being watched by his nemesis while he spends quality time with Ciorstaidh. Raonaid impresses the crofters with her veterinary skills, while Tormod reluctantly takes a new job on Seumas's boat.(n)

programme.title: Opry an Iúir

programme.title: Broadchurch
programme.desc: Out-of-town DI Alec Hardy investigates the mysterious death of an 11-year-old boy in an idyllic seaside community, aided by local girl DS Ellie Miller, who has her work cut out dealing with her unyielding new boss. Meanwhile, the sadness of losing their child consumes Beth and Mark Latimer as they and the other residents of Broadchurch try to cope with the media frenzy surrounding the case. programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help two more people who were found as babies after being abandoned to track down their birth families. Simon Jeffery was left in a box outside a railway tavern in Greenhithe, a story which hit the headlines but which he only learned about by chance at the age of 10. Fi Beazer was discovered in a public toilet in Warminster when she was only a few hours old. Both have spent their adult life trying to track down their parents, and submit DNA samples in the hope of finally getting the answers they seek.(n)

To make things easier, the players have four lifelines - Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend, 50:50 and Ask the Host - at their disposal.(n)

programme.title: Alexander Armstrong's Land of the Midnight Sun
programme.desc: The television presenter and comedian takes on an epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter, a spectacular but deadly time of year to see the unforgiving, ice-bound region. His odyssey is full of surprises as he witnesses natural wonders and discovers the magnetic lure of the far north. The first edition sees Alexander begin in Scandinavia and head toward Iceland, as he swims with locals in the ocean, faces the worst storm in 25 years, and goes down the world's first major tunnel to be built into the heart of a glacier.(n)

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside.(n)

programme.title: Absolutely India: Mancs in Mumbai
programme.desc: In the sixth and final episode, the brothers prepare to say their goodbyes to Mumbai, but before they leave, they are invited to appear on a local news report. For their final stop in the city, the boys meet 85-year-old Norma Otter, who was a team-mate of Dougie's international hockey star aunt, Pansy Thomas. Following the meeting, the boys decide to take on a junior girl's hockey team who are training nearby. As their adventure draws to a close, the family arrive in Jejuri in the midst of the 100,000 festival goers who are there to take part in what is known as the Golden Festival.(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: A special series looking back at Bradley and Barney's previous American adventures. The first episode sees the pair start in Los Angeles. Kicking things off in Venice beach, the pair get in shape with a game of volleyball and pump some weights at Muscle Beach, but the following the day Bradley is pushed to his limits when Barney reveals he has arranged for them to go skydiving. Leaving Los Angeles behind them, they head into the desert towards Arizona as they venture onto the famous Route 66, where the pair head off to Cowboy College.(n)

programme.title: Benidorm
programme.desc: Sheron and Billy Dawson check in for some pool-side fun, bringing along their teenage son Rob, young daughter Jodie, and Grandad Eddie, to claim the sun-loungers left vacant following the Garveys' departure from the vacation spot. Meanwhile, resort boss Joyce Temple-Savage is desperate for new staff, and must choose between efficient, goody two-shoes Amber and the outrageously flirtatious Marcus, and Troy returns to Benidorm with Jacqueline and Kenneth to scatter Donald's ashes in the place he loved best. Julie Graham, Steve Edge and Bobby Knutt join the cast of the comedy drama set in the sun-kissed Solana resort.(n)

programme.desc: The television presenter and comedian takes on an epic 8,000-mile journey halfway around the Arctic Circle deep in mid-winter, a spectacular but deadly time of year to see the unforgiving, ice-bound region. His odyssey is full of surprises as he witnesses natural wonders and discovers the magnetic lure of the far north. The first edition sees Alexander begin in Scandinavia and head toward Iceland, as he swims with locals in the ocean, faces the worst storm in 25 years, and goes down the world's first major tunnel to be built into the heart of a glacier.(n)

As the hunt for the guilty party intensifies, the detective discovers his son Andrew could be a suspect and is shocked when he hears he has been reported missing from his barracks. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, John Wood, Honeysuckle Weeks and Julian Ovenden.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is overjoyed when he discovers a cricketing legend has checked into the pub. David inadvertently prompts a petrol crisis. Nostalgic drama, starring Derek Fowlds, David Lonsdale and Clive Mantle.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is furious with Zoe for making the Tates a laughing stock. Emma gets drunk in the Woolpack and makes a scene. Zoe goes looking for Susie.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is convinced that Kim will come running back to him. Mandy comes up with a money-making scheme. Zoe is cornered into making a choice between Susie and Emma.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz urges Jim to apologise to Mike. Vera is daunted by the prospect of being a boss. Fiona realises is Fisher is legit when she discovers he is Raquel's agent.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Duckworths officially take over at the Rovers. Jim finds he isn't entitled to dole money as he was sacked. Fiona decides to have a go at modelling.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A wealthy banker goes missing while walking from his country house to the village post office. Poirot, who has acquired a parrot and developed a taste for conjuring tricks, bets the baffled Inspector Japp £5 he can solve the mystery without leaving his own apartment. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Blaketon is overjoyed when he discovers a cricketing legend has checked into the pub. David inadvertently prompts a petrol crisis. Nostalgic drama, starring Derek Fowlds, David Lonsdale and Clive Mantle.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The residents celebrate Christmas 1919 with a lavish party, but tensions are rife and Bates's arrest casts a shadow over the festivities. Mary has to consider her future with Sir Richard Carlisle following a disagreement at a shoot and Violet has concerns about Rosamund, whose new suitor - the dashing but raffish Lord Hepworth - is not all he seems. One-off festive special of the hit costume drama, guest starring Nigel Havers and Sharon Small, with Brendan Coyle, Michelle Dockery, Iain Glen, Maggie Smith and Samantha Bond.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)

programme.title: The Comedy Years
programme.desc: A look at comedy in 2003, when Tim, Lee and Dawn's love triangle reached a memorable conclusion in The Office and the original run of Cold Feet bowed out with a tragic storyline. It was also the year when members of the public found themselves the unwitting comedy stooges of hidden-camera shows like Trigger Happy TV and Three Non Blondes, Bruce Forsyth made a memorable appearance as guest host of Have I Got News for You and comedy legend Bob Monkhouse performed his last ever stand-up gig to an audience of fellow comics in a small London pub.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The peace of an Oxfordshire village is shattered by the apparently motiveless shooting of a woman through her kitchen window. Twenty-four hours later, her next-door neighbour suffers a similar fate. As Morse searches for a link between the victims, he becomes embroiled in an increasingly bitter election contest at an Oxford college. Starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Richard Briers, Maggie Steed and John Shrapnel.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: After resigning from his job as a traffic warden, henpecked George wonders how he can break the news to wife Mildred without incurring her wrath. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: When Larry decides to use someone else's name instead of his own, Robin finds himself entangled in a love triangle. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A farmer's murder and the crash landing of an enemy plane lead Foyle to investigate the activities of a group of land girls, and the detective begins to suspect the captured German airmen have something to hide. Stella Gonet and James Wilby guest star in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly patient's sudden death prompts a series of revelations about his decidedly shady past - and the raid he was planning on Ashfordly Bank. Meanwhile, Craddock is offered the chance of promotion, and Vernon embarks on yet another venture - Scripps' Roaming Holidays. Nostalgic drama, starring Philip Franks, Jason Durr and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma packs up her stuff. Zak gets a shock when he receives his phone bill. Terry feels relaxed before the big rugby match, confident that Martin Offiah will save the day.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe rushes to the hospital when she hears about Susie's accident. Terry wallows in self-pity after the rugby match defeat. Tina catches Chris going through Frank's personal files.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is adamant that her hotpot will be as good as Betty's. Raquel is pleased when Curly tells her Leo has been sacked. Vicky discovers Steve has hired a cleaner for their flat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack plays the breweries off each other to get lower beer prices. Steve accuses Vicky of trying to control him. Some dodgy pies at the Rovers give the pub's customers stomachaches.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin borrow Mr Roper's car to drive to Southampton for a football match. However, the vehicle has similar qualities to its unreliable owner and soon breaks down leaving the duo having to summon help from an unexpected quarter. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth contemplates retiring while on a trip to the Lake District, but shelves his plans when Hastings meets a woman whose collection of valuable miniature portraits has been stolen. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly patient's sudden death prompts a series of revelations about his decidedly shady past - and the raid he was planning on Ashfordly Bank. Meanwhile, Craddock is offered the chance of promotion, and Vernon embarks on yet another venture - Scripps' Roaming Holidays. Nostalgic drama, starring Philip Franks, Jason Durr and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Shirley MacLaine joins the cast of the period drama as Cora's mother Martha, who makes a grand entrance when she arrives at Downton for Matthew and Mary's wedding. However, with the couple struggling to stay united as their big day looms, will Martha's Atlantic crossing have been for nothing? Meanwhile, Mr Carson runs the rule over new footman Alfred, and Robert receives news that could leave him facing the greatest crisis of his life - and plunge the rest of the household into chaos. Starring Hugh Bonneville, Maggie Smith, Jim Carter and Michelle Dockery.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim leaves hospital and returns to Frank. Dave tries desperately to speak to Kim. Zak wants Mandy to rename her business to Zak's Snack Shack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff lays into Kim for destroying Dave. Terry gets ready for his lunch date with Elaine. Dave asks Kathy to go and see Kim for him and find out why she's back with Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy worries that Deirdre might be on the verge of a breakdown. Jack relishes being pampered by the brewery. Vera lays on a buffet for Daniel's christening tea.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin returns from holiday to find the garage shut and Jim sacked. Mike urges Deirdre to pull herself together. Reg is asked by the police to take part in an identity parade.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: With the estate's future hanging in the balance, Mary hopes to receive a bailout from her grandmother Martha - and with Violet's help, tries to make sure Downton puts on a bigger show than ever before with a grand party for friends and neighbours. Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes hears worrying news, and O'Brien's efforts to boost Alfred's progress meets with disapproval from Thomas. Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Shirley MacLaine and Maggie Smith.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie is left panicked when Andrea and Millie disappear, while Nate approaches Rhona with a proposition, suggesting they split half the restaurant order with Butlers. Brenda seeks an apology from Amelia for egging her window, but when Amelia maintains her innocence, Kerry tells Brenda to clear off.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie panics when Tracy reveals that Andrea left without her purse, cards, money or car. Kerry is pained as she overhears an emotional Amelia confessing to God that she egged Brenda's windows.(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly refuses to mention Raquel by name to Eric and Reg is furious as it is his only way of getting to Leo. Mike discovers Jim has disappeared with his car.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy and Betty return from their honeymoon, while Jim refuses to beg Mike for his job back.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank pressures Kim for an answer. Zoe and Emma's hen night arrives and Susie has one last attempt at changing Zoe's mind. The Dingles say goodbye to Elvis.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim is reluctant to let Dave announce their engagement around the village. Emma and Zoe make their vows to each other at the blessing. A fight erupts between Emma and Susie.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in Aidensfield, and Mike and Tricia try to reunite a blind woman with her stolen guide dog. Elsewhere, a young woman at the centre of a political scandal seeks refuge in Aidensfield as she attempts to avoid the paparazzi. Jason Durr and Clare Calbraith star.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Henpecked George finally finds a job, but the line of work could make him unpopular with road users. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)
