programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective is given a book on Victorian crimes while languishing in hospital, and soon becomes obsessed with a murder committed in 1859. Convinced the three men hanged for the crime were innocent, he sets out to solve the long-forgotten case. Starring John Thaw, Lisa Eichhorn, Judy Loe, Matthew Finney, James Grout and Clare Holman.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Vera is adamant that her hotpot will be as good as Betty's. Raquel is pleased when Curly tells her Leo has been sacked. Vicky discovers Steve has hired a cleaner for their flat.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack plays the breweries off each other to get lower beer prices. Steve accuses Vicky of trying to control him. Some dodgy pies at the Rovers give the pub's customers stomachaches.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma packs up her stuff. Zak gets a shock when he receives his phone bill. Terry feels relaxed before the big rugby match, confident that Martin Offiah will save the day.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zoe rushes to the hospital when she hears about Susie's accident. Terry wallows in self-pity after the rugby match defeat. Tina catches Chris going through Frank's personal files.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An elderly patient's sudden death prompts a series of revelations about his decidedly shady past - and the raid he was planning on Ashfordly Bank. Meanwhile, Craddock is offered the chance of promotion, and Vernon embarks on yet another venture - Scripps' Roaming Holidays. Nostalgic drama, starring Philip Franks, Jason Durr and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers babysit for their neighbours, but George misses his usual routine - so he nips out to a darts match with the infant in tow. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Eagle-eyed George sees an investment opportunity when Mildred suggests their dog should have puppies. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy's mother makes plans for her daughter's future happiness - and she knows just the man to help. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A crackdown on an organised crime racket leads Foyle and Milner to a shipyard owned by the Talbot brothers, a duo with dubious links to a communist agitator who has recently moved to the area. When an engineer is murdered just hours after working at the shipyard, the detectives desperately try to track down the killer and discover the truth surrounding the Talbots' shady business dealings. Peter Capaldi guest stars in the wartime drama, with Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim leaves hospital and returns to Frank. Dave tries desperately to speak to Kim. Zak wants Mandy to rename her business to Zak's Snack Shack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff lays into Kim for destroying Dave. Terry gets ready for his lunch date with Elaine. Dave asks Kathy to go and see Kim for him and find out why she's back with Frank.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy worries that Deirdre might be on the verge of a breakdown. Jack relishes being pampered by the brewery. Vera lays on a buffet for Daniel's christening tea.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin returns from holiday to find the garage shut and Jim sacked. Mike urges Deirdre to pull herself together. Reg is asked by the police to take part in an identity parade.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Troy is playing in an annual cricket match between Midsomer Worthy and Fletchers Cross when tragedy strikes. The wife of one of the team captains, a wealthy local landowner, is found battered to death with a cricket bat in a quarry. As Barnaby and Troy investigate, they discover no end of people with a grudge against the victim's husband - who may have been complicit in some other suspicious deaths. Guest starring Imelda Staunton and Robert Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: New sergeant Dennis Merton arrives at Ashfordly police station and soon finds himself embroiled in a CID stakeout - but Ventress's shady past threatens the entire operation. Meanwhile, all efforts to pummel David into the peak of physical fitness look set to come to nothing, until a chance encounter knocks Vernon for six. Nostalgic drama, starring William Simons and Duncan Bell.(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: With the estate's future hanging in the balance, Mary hopes to receive a bailout from her grandmother Martha - and with Violet's help, tries to make sure Downton puts on a bigger show than ever before with a grand party for friends and neighbours. Meanwhile, Mrs Hughes hears worrying news, and O'Brien's efforts to boost Alfred's progress meets with disapproval from Thomas. Costume drama, starring Michelle Dockery, Shirley MacLaine and Maggie Smith.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: American GIs arrive in Hastings to a mixed welcome and Foyle is called on to calm the locals. But his attempts at soothing relations are jeopardised when a barmaid's involvement with a visiting soldier results in murder, leading the detective to defy orders to keep the peace and solve the case. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks and Keith Barron.(n)

programme.title: Sporting Funnies
programme.desc: A short sports film depicting extraordinary British Touring Car crashes and smashes.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A microchip found in a dead Navy officer's body sparks off another tricky investigation for Quincy, who struggles to make sense of events.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo agonises over whether to accept the offer of a job with a leading law firm, when a series of recent drug-related deaths begins to overshadow his decision.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A desperate girl contacts Holmes and asks him to clear her boyfriend of killing his father, a farmer found bludgeoned to death beside a lake. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett , Peter Vaughan and James Purefoy.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur becomes a rebel without a clue by launching a motorbike despatch agency - but Ray is concerned the entire venture is heading for a crash. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An expert sniper with a high-powered imported rifle holds a family hostage in a high-rise flat, planning to target a royal visitor at the Wimbledon tennis tournament. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A nine-year-old girl is shot dead in a feud between rival gangs, and Quincy finds himself caught up in an argument about how to deal with juvenile offenders.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo investigates the case of a surgeon in desperate need of money to pay off enormous gambling debts. The physician is breaking the law by recommending to his patients they undergo expensive and unnecessary operations at risk to their own lives - while thugs pressure the families of the victims to pay for the dangerous treatment.(n)

programme.title: Championship League Snooker Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day eight from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring matches in Group 6 - involving Ali Carter, Matthew Selt, Sam Craigie and Dominic Dale, and Group 11, which comprises Mark Allen, Martin O'Donnell, Michael White and Nigel Bond. Featuring 64-players in round-robin groups, the new-look Championship League is taking place behind closed doors with strict rules on social distancing, Covid testing and hygiene in place, in line with government guidelines. Marshall Arena has been selected as the venue as it has an on-site accommodation meaning no players, officials or staff will need to leave the venue once they have entered. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Alan McManus, Stephen Hendry and Neal Foulds.(n)

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Newcomer Steve Hursey goes on his first winter run - and there is a lot riding on it. His family's sea-to-plate restaurant is low on lobsters stocks and it is his job to re-fill them. He is fishing around Hunter Island, an area known for giant reds and howling westerly winds, but weather is the least of his worries. The rope line to one of his pots is tangled, and it's threatening to pull deckhand Jamie overboard.(n)

programme.title: Death Wish V: The Face of Death (1994)
programme.desc: A former vigilante tries to settle down and start a new life under an assumed name with his fashion designer fiancee, who is being menaced by gangsters. When the thugs disfigure and ultimately murder her and the police are helpless to act, he breaks out his guns one more time. Thriller sequel, with Charles Bronson, Lesley-Anne Down, Robert Joy and Michael Parks.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes

Victorian detective drama, guest starring Anthony Valentine with Jeremy Brett.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is offered the chance to buy a cheap Daimler, but while trying to locate its dealer's elusive premises is arrested for kerb-crawling. With a gross of red light bulbs in his boot, the evidence seems compelling, and even Ray begins to doubt his uncle's innocence. Nevertheless, the loyal nephew sets out to track down the so-called Goodtime garage to provide corroboration. Lighthearted drama, with George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A visiting American senator is assassinated in broad daylight. After a newspaper publishes the names of witnesses, Bodie and Doyle are assigned to protect them, but the task soon gets out of hand. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The university branch of a bank is robbed, and though Regan and the Flying Squad catch most of the gang a known killer and his sidekick remain inside holding the manager and two women hostage. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner performs an autopsy when a man demands answers over the death of his brother, who died following an operation by an eminent surgeon.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo accuses a district attorney of corruption for failing to convict his friend's killer. He is suspended from duty for the outburst, so sets out to get justice on his own.(n)

programme.title: Championship League Snooker Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day nine from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring matches in Winners Group C and Winners Group D. At this stage of the tournament, only the players who finished top of the original 16 groups remain. Here, they are split into four further groups of four, with two groups per day played over two tables. The new-look Championship League is taking place behind closed doors with strict rules on social distancing, Covid testing and hygiene in place, in line with government guidelines. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Alan McManus, Stephen Hendry and Neal Foulds.(n)

programme.title: Waterworld (1995)
programme.desc: In the future, environmental disasters have left the planet flooded. A web-footed mutant mariner helps a woman and an orphan girl search for a mythical haven of dry land, with the help of a map tattooed on the child's back - but in the process has to fend off a villainous crew of nicotine-addicted pirates. Sci-fi adventure, starring Kevin Costner, Dennis Hopper, Jeanne Tripplehorn, Tina Majorino and Rick Aviles.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Touched by the plight of a woman suffering from a degenerative nerve disease, Quincy decides to fight the government and drug companies to ensure she gets the medication she needs.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Shelley guest stars as Evelyn McNeil, the widowed sister-in-law of Captain Frank McNeil. programme.title: Championship League Snooker Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day 10 from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring matches in Winners Group A and Winners Group B. At this stage of the tournament, only the players who finished top of the original 16 groups remain. Here, they are split into four further groups of four, with two groups per day played over two tables. The new-look Championship League is taking place behind closed doors with strict rules on social distancing, Covid testing and hygiene in place, in line with government guidelines. Marshall Arena has been selected as the venue as it has an on-site accommodation meaning no players, officials or staff will need to leave the venue once they have entered. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Alan McManus, Stephen Hendry and Neal Foulds.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: A look at how sole eyewitness Joanne Lees came under immense scrutiny and pressure from both the British and Australian press as they hounded her continuously for an interview, while certain detectives were also sceptical of her version of events. The programme includes detailed sections of a video showing Joanne being confronted by detectives about seeming inconsistences in her account of the attack.(n)

programme.title: Women Who Kill
programme.desc: With more than 30,000 women in prisons in America serving lengthy sentences for murder, this documentary looks at some of the extraordinary stories of women who committed these crimes, and the reasons why they killed people they knew and loved. They include Amber Hilberling, who was 19 and pregnant when she pushed her husband out of a window, Patricia Ignacio, who admitted to bludgeoning her cousin to death with a rock, and Ana Trujillo, who murdered her boyfriend by stabbing him with a stiletto shoe.(n)

Will a bit of witty repartee and the revelation about his own furry friend encourage her to let her guard down? Meanwhile, James has extended his stay in the hope of meeting his soulmate - and it seems that 6ft 6 Max from Oxford might just be the man for him.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. In this first episode, Andrew Fraser, formerly one of Australia's foremost criminal defence lawyers, begins the reinvestigation into the Peter Falconio story, visits the Outback crime scene and speaks to a leading forensic scientist and witnesses from the time of the murder, who raise serious questions about evidence in the case.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Britain's Coronavirus Catastrophe: Did the Government Get it Wrong? Dispatches
programme.desc: Examining the the British government's approach to dealing with the coronavirus pandemic. With Britain's confirmed death rate from coronavirus now one of the highest in the world, and the Government's strategy to get the economy restarted and prevent a second spike still developing, Dispatches reporter Antony Barnett begins asking questions that may shed a light on how to beat the virus in the future. With the input of people at the heart of events, Barnett investigates the eight astonishing weeks that saw the UK move into lockdown, and reveals the key decisions and moments that led to the UK shutting down and that have determined what has happened since.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: New Zealand's Northern Explorer, the longest-running passenger service in the country. The train travels 423 miles along the historic North Island Main Trunk Line on a 12-hour voyage and the journey takes travellers through some of the most astonishing landscapes of the southern hemisphere from volcanoes to coast and from lava fields to unspoilt islands. Historians explain the history of the railway and tell the tale of the terrible tragedy at Tangiwai, when over 150 people were killed by a volcanic mudflow that hit the train. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb investigates the origins of the obsession for mass witch-hunts and trials that swept the British Isles during the 17th century. Travelling to Scotland and Denmark, she challenges the accepted wisdom that King James VI of Scotland, later James I of England, was responsible for stirring up the hysteria. She also tells the story of Scotland's first mass witch trial in 1590 and the infamous Pendle witch trials towards the end of his reign.(n)

programme.desc: James Herriot was the original Yorkshire Vet, and this documentary sees Peter Wright and Julian Norton celebrating how the Herriot ethos and legacy lives on in veterinary practice and across the North Yorkshire countryside.(n)

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programme.desc: Ben Fogle reunites with a couple living off the west coast of Australia, and discovers the logistical nightmares they have overcome to make a home for themselves on a desert island, as well as enduring the extremes of the Australian climate. While they have recently had cause to celebrate, their lives are becoming increasingly difficult, which may result in them moving on.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.desc: Behind the doors of the Heinz factory in Wigan, where 850 people work around the clock to produce 200 different products, including soup, pasta and, of course, 450 millions of cans of baked beans every year. The documentary discovers the secrets of making food on this scale, meets workers including food tasters, apprentice engineers and the factory's own firemen, and also learns the story behind the famous Heinz 57 number.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Shocking Ads with Shane Richie
programme.desc: Part one of two. The actor showcases some of the most memorable adverts from around the world, including Japan, Brazil, France, Belgium, Norway and the USA. He also opens up the archives to show vintage commercials from yesteryear. The first edition features skydiving cats selling insurance, some killer bathroom soap, pensioners behaving badly and unicorns on the toilet.(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels 1,500 miles in a journey from Cape Town in South Africa to the Victoria Falls in Zimbabwe, following a hugely ambitious railway pioneered by 19th-century British colonial politician Cecil Rhodes. He starts his trip in luxury on the Blue Train, a five-star hotel on rails that takes him to the mining town of Kimberley, where he explores the diamond mine where Rhodes made his fortune.(n)

programme.title: Witches: A Century of Murder
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb's investigation into the notorious 17th-century witch trials concludes with a profile of Matthew Hopkins, the opportunistic puritan witch-hunter who transformed paranoia about Satanism into a lucrative business as he hanged hundreds of people for crimes they could not possibly have committed. Making use of contemporary reports, Suzannah paints a portrait of a man who believed he could cross any boundary and break any law to achieve his goals. Naming himself 'Witchfinder General', Hopkins toured the country charging fees for his services, but Suzannah's research reveals that ultimately, the public's distaste for witches was not as strong as its hatred of being conned by charlatans. Hopkins would eventually die of tuberculosis in relative obscurity, but his malign legacy lived on across the Atlantic, as witch-hunts began in Salem, Massachusetts.(n)

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Chris travels along the route of the Soviet-era Trans-Caucasus Railway, beginning in the Azerbaijani capital of Baku. He then heads into Georgia, exploring Tbilisi's vintage metro system and funicular railway, before travelling to the holiday resort of Borjomi, where world-class engineering is on display in a mountain rail bridge designed by Gustave Eiffel.(n)

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: An insight into the working life of officers patrolling the nation's roads, using modern technology and old-fashioned methods to bring criminals to justice.(n)

programme.title: The Railway 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary series offering a behind the scenes look at life working at TransPennine Express, from control room to maintenance depot, and drivers to cleaners. In the first episode, a derailed train threatens to bring the network to a halt during the busiest time of the year. Meanwhile at HQ, the bosses decide to bring in a new timetable which sees hundreds of services scrapped.(n)

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: A 41-year-old patient is escorted off the premises of Hull Royal Infirmary after verbally abusing a receptionist in the waiting room, but is allowed back into the hospital to have his hand treated after calming down. In resus, Dr Jeremy Osman and a full trauma team attempt to save the life of 18-year-old Jack, who has severe injuries after falling off his scooter at high speed.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Outback
programme.desc: An investigation into events which led to the deaths of travellers Mia Ayliffe-Chung and Tom Jackson. It's alleged that Smail Ayad, a 29-year-old French backpacker, killed them both at a hostel in Queensland, Australia on August 23, 2016. Featuring testimonies from family members and witnesses.(n)

programme.title: Hondo (1953)
programme.desc: A hard-boiled cavalry scout with a feral dog as companion stumbles across a woman and her child stranded on their homestead deep inside hostile territory. Despite repeated attacks by the Apaches, they refuse to leave their home, leaving the scout with no choice but to stay and defend her. Western, starring John Wayne, Geraldine Page, Ward Bond and Michael Pate.(n)

programme.title: Anastasia (1956)
programme.desc: In 1920s Paris, exiled Russians claim they have found the daughter of the last Tsar, who was originally believed to have been killed with the rest of her family. She is taken into the care of a General and groomed until she can convince Anastasia's cousin Prince Paul that she is genuinely the tsarina and inherit a fortune. Historical drama, starring an Oscar-winning Ingrid Bergman, and Yul Brynner.(n)

programme.title: Death Drums Along the River (1963)
programme.desc: A British inspector is called to Africa to investigate the murder of a fellow policeman and uncovers a diamond-smuggling operation seemingly connected to an upriver hospital. Mystery, based on Edgar Wallace's novel Sanders on the River, starring Richard Todd, Marianne Koch, Albert Lieven and Walter Rilla.(n)

programme.title: 49th Parallel (1941)
programme.desc: Six Nazis are left stranded in Canada after their U-boat is sunk. As they try to escape the attentions of the authorities, they struggle to win sympathy among the locals. But when that fails, they decide to flee to the still-neutral USA instead. Michael Powell's Oscar-winning Second World War thriller, starring Anton Walbrook, Leslie Howard, Laurence Olivier, Glynis Johns and Niall MacGinnis.(n)

programme.title: Fantastic Four: Rise of the Silver Surfer (2007)
programme.desc: The super-powered team takes on a mysterious alien who has been sent by his planet-eating master to prepare Earth for obliteration. While the four heroes try to find a way to foil its plans, an old enemy returns, intent on seizing the extraterrestrial's power. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Michael Chiklis, Chris Evans and Jessica Alba, with the voice of Laurence Fishburne.(n)

programme.title: True Lies (1994)
programme.desc: A seemingly ordinary computer salesman leads a double life as a secret agent battling international terrorism. His naive wife is unaware of his dual employment, and goes in search of adventure - only to end up in the clutches of terrorists who have stolen a nuclear weapon. James Cameron's action comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis and Tom Arnold.(n)

programme.title: Adore (2013)
programme.desc: Two women who have been friends all their lives live in a quiet stretch of the Australian coast. They find unexpected happiness after each has an affair with the other's teenage son, but their unusual relationships are threatened when the outside world intrudes on their isolation. Drama based on Doris Lessing's novel The Grandmothers, with Naomi Watts and Robin Wright.(n)

programme.title: Me and You (2012)
programme.desc: A troubled teenager wants to shut out the world, so tells his parents he is going on a school trip while actually planning to spend a week hiding in a cellar. However, his solitude is soon invaded by his drug-addicted older step-sister. Bernardo Bertolucci's drama, with Jacopo Olmo Antinori and Tea Falco. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: The Black Tent (1956)
programme.desc: A wounded British Army captain is saved by a tribe of nomads in the Libyan desert during the Second World War, and they take him to their camp, where he is cared for by the sheikh's daughter and falls in love with her. Romantic drama, with Anthony Steel, Anna-Maria Sandri, Donald Sinden, Andre Morell and Donald Pleasence.(n)

programme.title: In Harm's Way (1965)
programme.desc: Two Navy officers are disgraced in the aftermath of the attack on Pearl Harbour, one for a disastrous engagement with the enemy and the other for a drunken brawl after learning of his wife's death. They get another chance to prove their worth when they are placed in charge of a vital counter-offensive. Second World War drama, starring John Wayne, Kirk Douglas and Henry Fonda.(n)

programme.title: Anna and the King (1999)
programme.desc: A widowed schoolmistress leaves Victorian England to tutor a son of the King of Siam, only to discover his majesty actually expects her to care for all 58 of his children. In spite of disagreements and many ups and downs, the teacher gradually forms a deep platonic love for her royal employer. Period drama, starring Jodie Foster, Chow Yun-Fat, Ling Bai, Syed Alwi, Tom Felton and Randall Duk Kim.(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee (1986)
programme.desc: A New York reporter searches the Australian Outback for a maverick hunter with a legendary reputation, and invites the adventurer back to America. But the die-hard Aussie needs to draw on all his survival skills to adjust to life in the urban jungle. Comedy adventure, written by and starring Paul Hogan, with Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Mark Blum.(n)

programme.title: Logan (2017)
programme.desc: In the near future, Wolverine cares for an ailing Professor X somewhere on the Mexican border. However, the razor-clawed hero's attempts to hide from the world are turned upside down when a young mutant arrives, pursued by lethal forces. Fantasy adventure, starring Hugh Jackman, Patrick Stewart, Dafne Keen and Stephen Merchant.(n)

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish.(n)

programme.title: Night Moves (2013)
programme.desc: A farmer, a high-school drop-out and an ex-soldier form a militant environmentalist group, and plot to blow up a controversial dam. While their plan is successful, it has many shocking consequences, and they are faced with a series of moral dilemmas in the aftermath. Thriller, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Dakota Fanning and Peter Sarsgaard.(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Seabees (1944)
programme.desc: Construction workers battle against Japanese forces while working with the US navy in the South Pacific during the Second World War, while the head of the company and an officer compete for the affections of a local woman. However, when one recklessly causes a tragedy, he seeks redemption by launching an attack on a tank battalion. Drama, starring John Wayne, Susan Hayward and Dennis O'Keefe.(n)

programme.title: Strategic Air Command (1955)
programme.desc: A pilot relishes the opportunity to return to the baseball pitch once the Second World War is over. However, his sporting career is put on hold when he's drafted back into the US Air Force and given the opportunity to fly a revolutionary new jet. Drama, starring James Stewart, Frank Lovejoy, Barry Sullivan, Alex Nicol and June Allyson.(n)

programme.title: Shane (1953)
programme.desc: A mysterious gunfighter intervenes when a man and his family are threatened by a predatory cattle baron determined to force every homesteader in the valley off the land. The situation rapidly escalates into violence, while an unspoken attraction develops between the gunman and the settler's wife. Western, starring Alan Ladd, Jack Palance, Van Heflin and Jean Arthur. Based on the novel by Jack Schaefer.(n)

programme.title: Transformers: Dark of the Moon (2011)
programme.desc: The heroic autobots discover that a spaceship from their own planet crashed into the moon decades ago, carrying their former leader and an incredibly powerful device. They must fight to defend the human race as their enemies the decepticons plot to use this machine to rebuild their own world and destroy the Earth. Sci-fi adventure sequel, with Shia LaBeouf, Josh Duhamel and Rosie Huntington-Whiteley.(n)

programme.title: The Square (2017)
programme.desc: A prestigious Stockholm museum's chief art curator finds himself in times of both professional and personal crisis as he attempts to set up a controversial new exhibit, known as The Square. Comedy drama, starring Claes Bang, Elisabeth Moss and Dominic West.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina's plan to have Zelda nominated for Professor of the Year goes awry when the committee discovers that she attended college in 1873 and has her fired from her job.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina decides to take matters into her own hands when she gets an eerie feeling that she is being followed, ending up face to face with her mother.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe puts a ban on the use of all mobiles and computers over the weekend in the hope of improving communication within the family. However, he soon comes to regret his decision when he is offered a new job and needs to get back to his prospective employer in time to accept the post.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe is furious when he finds out that Dani had some older friends over to the house who drank his expensive bottle of wine, but wonders whether she is being totally honest with him about the event. When Lennox expresses admiration for her college professor Evita, Mel grows jealous of their relationship, until Evita asks to interview Mel for her latest research project. Shalita Grant guest stars alongside Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.desc: Now that she knows the truth about Margot, Riley decides to look for her biological father too, and uses her parents' high-school yearbook as a guide. With a little help from Ben, she narrows down the possible candidates to an easy-going pastry chef and a high-flying lawyer. Comedy, starring Chelsea Kane and Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley looks forward to meeting Philip's sister when she visits from England, but her excitement vanishes when it becomes clear his sibling is unimpressed with his taste in girlfriends. Meanwhile, Danny's contract negotiations with the Rangers are put in jeopardy. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is hurt when Professor Proton (Bob Newhart) contacts Leonard for help, so he befriends TV scientist Bill Nye, and Raj gets jealous when Howard crashes a girls' night in making home-made jewellery. Starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki, and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry makes fun of Adam for taking part in a school musical production, but when he finds out that Adam and Lainey have been cast in roles that require them to kiss, he tries to sabotage it by joining the stage crew. Meanwhile, substitute teacher Beverly causes chaos in Erica's class. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta hits it off with Sophia, a woman he meets in a bar, only to find out she is the defence attorney representing a perpetrator he is trying to put behind bars. Diaz pressures Santiago to stand as a union rep, while Gina and Boyle try to move on from their relationship. Eva Longoria (best known as Desperate Housewives' Gaby) joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct goes into lockdown on Thanksgiving when a mysterious powder-filled package is delivered to the building. Peralta, who has been left in charge while Holt and Jeffords are out of the office, is confident he can keep morale high and avoid any chaos - but things soon spiral out of control.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard are furious when they learn that Sheldon went to work with the military behind their backs. Angered, the pair ask Kripke to help them get their own back on Sheldon, much to his annoyance. Meanwhile, Bernadette suspects that Ruchi might be trying to steal her job while she is on bed rest, so she asks Raj to investigate for her, putting him in a very awkward position. Dean Norris guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys find a laptop that has some virtual currency on it. The only snag is, the computer belongs to Penny's ex-boyfriend Zack. Meanwhile, a seven-year-old video reveals a secret about Leonard and Penny's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel launches a healthy diet campaign while running for a Senate seat, but a voter soon catches her out eating some unhealthy fast food. When the man tweets a photo of her gorging on a burger and calls her a hypocrite, she gets into a war of words with him.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel receives unexpected news that could have a direct impact on her attempt to run for a senate seat. Ryder receives his orders from the US Navy, and a car crash gives Lenin and Zander a new outlook on life. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.desc: Riley doubts her future with Ben after a psychic reading suggests their relationship is doomed. Ben tries to convince her that they are soulmates, but realises he knows very little about what makes her happy. Tucker does his best to persuade Bonnie to move out of the flat, while Danny asks Bonnie's ex-boyfriend Brad to help him cover up the failure of his Paris adventure.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Danny saves the day when Ben and Riley are mugged - but cannot take any of the credit because he is still pretending that he lives in Paris. Tucker realises that such a great human interest story gives him the perfect opportunity to save his job on Mary Hart's TV show, so he invites Ben and Riley to appear on the programme to talk about their experience. Mary Hart guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Peralta notices Holt is in a bad mood, he teams up with the captain's husband Kevin to find out what has gone wrong. Boyle tries to get a confession out of an elderly bank-robbery suspect (played by Happy Days creator Garry Marshall), who pretends to be frail and adorable whenever Santiago and Diaz are around.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: As the wedding of Gina's mother and Boyle's father arrives, each member of the squad is given a task to complete for the big day. However, Peralta and Santiago get sidetracked chasing a criminal, and Jeffords struggles to officiate at the ceremony. Comedy, guest starring Sandra Bernhard and Nick Cannon.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry plans big romantic gestures for Lainey on high school prom night, while Erica and Geoff try to attend the event together, but things don't go as planned. Adam is thrilled when a nerdy but cute girl asks him out, but is horrified when Beverly gets involved.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry attempts to drive a wedge between Adam and Pops when they debate the merits of a recently released Batman movie. Erica deliberately makes a mess of her interview at one university so that she will only receive an offer from her first choice of college, but her plan goes badly awry. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Sean Giambrone and Hayley Orrantia.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Meanwhile, Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valour before midnight - a challenge that prompts him to don his own selection of disguises.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride

programme.sub-title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Ant Middleton gets his training staff to set the recruits a series of challenges designed to expose their weaknesses. An expedition up the side of a mountain quickly takes its toll, with one celebrity contestant collapsing and another contracting hypothermia in the extreme cold.(n)

programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. First up is 27-year-old flight attendant Alex from Bristol. Having never had a serious relationship, he is hoping to land himself a man for the long haul. He is followed by 26-year-old Laura from Gloucestershire, a horse-loving country girl who is looking for love.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty

In the first episode, missing for nearly 20 years, drunken scientist Rick arrives on his daughter Beth's doorstep looking to move in, but her husband Jerry isn't too thrilled. Rick converts the garage into his laboratory and involves easily influenced grandson Morty in his insane misadventures, starting with a mission to another dimension to find the seeds to mega-trees.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty

Twenty-eight-year-old property manager and glamorous burlesque dancer Becky was once a shy teenager, but her raunchy dance routines have boosted her self-esteem, and her date is with Bristolian Lewis, a budding comedian who is tired of the single life, or as he puts it 'juggling with one ball'.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Hilda is on the rebound when she falls in love with a train conductor and plans to get married. But Sabrina and Zelda believe the new relationship is moving far too quickly.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel does her best to talk Lennox out of going to Colombia on a research expedition with her college professor, while Joe makes Dani call her mother to confess about her recent wine-drinking escapades. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Ryder announces that he plans to join the US Navy, but Mel and Joe are unconvinced that he is prepared for military life, and come up with a plan to make him change his mind. Their plot involves Joe pretending to support the youngster's decision by giving him a taste of what to expect during his training, but instead devising a fiendish mock boot camp intended to persuade him the life of a serviceman is not for him. Comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joe Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley gives Ben and Danny the time capsules they made at school, which causes everyone to reflect on what they have achieved in their lives so far. Meanwhile Bonnie is not happy when some unwanted attendees turn up at her dance class. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben is disappointed when he misses Emma's first steps so Bonnie, Tucker and Riley try to recreate the moment for him. However, the activity causes the group to contemplate how much their lives have changed since the child's arrival. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin.(n)

programme.desc: Peralta tries to add a touch of romance to an event when he invites his girlfriend Sophia (Eva Longoria) to the precinct's work trip, along with Amy's boyfriend, Teddy (Kyle Bornheimer). programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy try to eliminate the stress from their wedding planning by applying mathematics to the process. Raj realises best friend Howard is actually hurting his confidence.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Howard plan respective birthday celebrations for Amy and Halley. A letter from his brother makes Leonard feel anxious about his own lack of accomplishments. The series takes a mid-season break until the new year.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: First episode of the family comedy starring Melissa Joan Hart as a local politician left to look after her niece and nephew after a family scandal, who is forced to seek the help of a male nanny to help her cope with her new responsibilities. With Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Male nanny Joey moves in to make his childcare work easier, but his 56-inch flat-screen TV disrupts the status quo of the household. Meanwhile, Mel sets her sights on winning a date with a man she meets at work. Family comedy, starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: A nervous Danny is offered a cameo role on a popular daytime TV show - and when he charms one of the leading actresses, she decides to make him her new love interest. Tucker and Ben come up with an inventive way to get Bonnie out of their flat and back into her own.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Bonnie gets depressed about all the losers she has been dating, so Ben and Danny compete to see who can pair her up with the perfect guy. Determined to beat his big brother at something, Ben arranges for Bonnie to date one of his customers, while Danny sets her up with Ben's boss. Meanwhile, Riley and Tucker find themselves involved in their neighbour Sondra's marital problems. Starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake's seldom-seen father Roger arrives to spend some time with his son, but Charles is sceptical of his true motives. Meanwhile, Holt tests Amy, Terry, Gina and Rosa with a near-impossible brain teaser in exchange for tickets to a Beyonce concert. Guest starring Bradley Whitford (The West Wing).(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Santiago and Diaz investigate Jake's claim that somebody is trying to sabotage his professional life and get him dismissed. Boyle partners up with Hitchcock and Scull to solve a tough case, while Jeffords lies to Gina claiming he and Holt attended her dance troupe's performance. Chris Parnell guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray is honoured as one of the area's top businessmen, Beverly's attempt to help her husband find a hobby backfires, and Erica comes to the rescue when Barry's scheme to steal golf carts goes awry.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.sub-title: The Day After The Day After
programme.desc: A scary TV movie motivates Barry to beg his father to build an underground bunker for the family, but Murray isn't easily persuaded. Erica and Adam go over the top with lavish Mother's Day gifts for Beverly before bringing important requests to her. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon takes the guys to a cabin in the middle of nowhere to meet a reclusive scientist. Penny and Bernadette improvise Amy's bachelorette party. Peter MacNicol guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon isn't pleased by the newfound popularity of Stuart's comic book shop when an author puts the place on the map. When Raj attempts to take the credit for Penny's astronomical discovery, friendships are tested. Howard and Bernadette try to arrange a date night.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake makes an arrest without much proof, and so has to spend the next 48 hours collecting enough evidence to keep his suspect in custody. While his co-workers are forced to help him out over the weekend, Charles judges a pie contest between Rosa and Gina, and the Captain gives advice to Terry, who has a problem involving his brother-in-law. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Peralta and Diaz are framed for a crime they didn't commit, it's up to the other members of the Nine-Nine to find out what really happened and try to save them from going to jail.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Holt asks for Jake's help in a case that is exceptionally meaningful to him. Charles and Terry work together on a side business.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will is left home alone when his mother goes away for the weekend with an old school friend, so he decides to take advantage of the situation. After a wild time with the boys ordering items off the internet and raiding the fridge, he wakes up to an unpleasant shock. Comedy, starring Simon Bird and Joe Thomas.(n)

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Will decides to organise one last adventure for his friends before they all go their separate ways, so the gang heads off for a camping trip in the countryside. While Will draws on all his scout training, Jay, Neil and Simon are not prepared for the great outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: It's July 12th and the Quinn family are rushing to get out of town before the Orange Parades start. They are packed and nearly ready to go when James, Michelle and Clare arrive as unexpected members of the escape party. To make matters worse, Aunt Sarah has recently started reading tarot cards and is predicting disaster for this trip - and it seems that for once, she might just be right. The only question is, when and how will things go wrong? Comedy, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Jamie-Lee O'Donnell, Dylan Llewellyn and Nicola Coughlan.(n)

programme.title: Derry Girls
programme.desc: Erin is over the moon when she manages to secure herself the editorship of the school magazine, The Habit. Especially when she uncovers an explosive story. Orla become obsessed by step aerobics, Da Gerry gets into trouble with Ma Mary and love is in the air for Aunt Sarah. Comedy, starring Saoirse-Monica Jackson, Louisa Harland, Tommy Tiernan, Tara Lynne O'Neill and Kathy Kiera Clarke.(n)

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Buffy is saved from three of the Master's henchmen by the mysterious Angel, who bravely fends them off and spends the night guarding her while she sleeps - but she is later distraught to discover that he is actually a vampire. Meanwhile, Joyce fall prey to the villainous Darla. With Sarah Michelle Gellar and David Boreanaz.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Jerry gets everyone to ditch their mobile phones and tablets so that the family can really bond when his parents pay a holiday visit and bring along a new friend. Meanwhile, Rick builds a tiny amusement park inside the body of his homeless friend Ruben, but when problems arise he must shrink Morty down and inject him into the site in an attempt to save Ruben's life. Special Christmas episode guest starring Dana Carvey and John Oliver.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: The duo try to get to the bottom of a twisted mystery when aliens transport them into a familiar but alternate reality created in a huge simulation chamber on board their spaceship. An oblivious Jerry is accidentally included in the experiment but fails to notice the subtle changes in his surroundings as he makes a big advertising pitch at work. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Sabrina persuades Miles to get his driver's license and finds herself in the middle of a nasty lawsuit.(n)

programme.title: Rules of Engagement

programme.desc: Sabrina begins a career in journalism with her first paying job at Scorch, an alternative music magazine that focuses on everything and anything cutting-edge.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe fears that being a nanny will prevent him from having a love life, so a worried Mel lies to get him a date with her former sorority sister. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel disapproves of a boy Lennox has started dating, despite seeing 'a bad boy' herself. Meanwhile, Joe takes it upon himself to tidy his employer's closet. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Vegetarians Malcolm and Fiona Piers-Taylor host at the Old Mill House B&B in Bridport, Dorset, and both come under attack at dinner when Malcolm voices his opinions on meat eating. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike and Denise Mitchell welcome the contestants to Beaches B&B in Paignton, Devon, which only opened for business a few weeks ago. Fellow competitor Katherine puts the owners through their paces at breakfast when she asks for something off the menu, and the hosts are pleasantly surprised at feedback time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together for one last time to discover what they have been paid, and the identity of the winner. The Oak owner Natalie wants to know why some of her guests were not enthused by her extractor fans, but she herself receives aggression from Dennis over her criticism of the White Lady. Katherine and Stuart defend their vegetarian breakfast, and Mike and Denise deal with negative comments about their cereal selection.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Nantwich and Stoke-on-Trent, where Henry comes across a 1960s moped, and Simon restores an early Mirror Dinghy.(n)

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with psychologist Linda Papadopoulos joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The plumbers race to install basic heating and water as Dick, Angel and the Strawbridge family decide it's time to move into their crumbling chateau. They'll be sharing their new home with bats, birds and thousands of flies, but Angel is in love with their new home and the clan begins the process of adjusting to French country life.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of architectural designer Lincoln Miles and his wife, artist Lisa Traxler, who want to create their own home on the Isle of Wight. They have bought a property on a prime piece of land and set out to convert it into a creative structure that harmonises with the unspoilt woodland setting.(n)

programme.title: Prince Philip: The Plot to Make a King
programme.desc: Secret History documentary telling the inside story of the dynastic tensions that were unleashed when Princess Elizabeth, the future queen, fell in love with Prince Philip. It was felt that Philip was rough and ill-mannered - and with the Second World War still fresh in most people's minds, the royal and political elite disliked his German roots. But following the death of King George VI in 1952, the establishment feared a dynastic coup, with the Queen torn between the two sides. The documentary examines the complexities and frustrations of the royal marriage, as the monarch struggled to reconcile the love of her husband with the suspicions of her family and her government.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Headteacher Emma arrives in resus having been in a collision with a bus while cycling home from the school she works at. As she is treated, her husband Mick reflects on being informed what had happened. Meanwhile, 33-year-old Josh is treated for severe back pain, and 87-year-old Pamela is examined for a possible blood clot in her leg, and tells the doctors about her childhood in London during the Blitz.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 79-year-old man arrives in hospital after collapsing in the street whilst walking his dog, and doctors give him medication to try and stabilise his very high heart rate. Meanwhile, a young boy is treated after pushing a plastic toy down his ear, and doctors show concern for a man who has numbness in his arm and little finger, and send him for a CT scan.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.desc: Four famous faces go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games across the week, with comedians Nish Kumar and Al Murray, Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo and broadcaster Anneka Rice the first contestants. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the weekly scores are tallied resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday, when the victor chooses between a House of Games dartboard and a branded dressing gown.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: King John's Ruin - Peak District
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The stories behind closed roads, including a route once used by the French Resistance and areas made impassable by crumbling hills and sand dunes.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: A serial killer who terrorised London in 1942, using the chaos of the Blitz as an opportunity to attack women.(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A secret weapons program in the heart of Austria that could have ended badly for the Allies - and may have indicated nuclear plans being concocted by Hitler.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The desert town that faced a disaster of biblical proportions. Plus, scorched remains that speak of imprisonment and torture and vast coastal ruins that reveal a past once wiped from history.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds a very rare 70s Sunbeam Lotus that has been rotting away in a farmyard for 29 years, while a Morris Minor convertible goes under the hammer.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: The underground base with the power to destroy a nation, the site linked with an attempt to kill a vicious dictator, and an ancient world discovered in the open American wilderness.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Balmoral, where she takes a tour of the grounds in a 1953 land rover and views archive footage of the royal family. Valuables brought in to be viewed include a collection of medals from both World Wars presented to a man who also rescued survivors from the Titanic, an art nouveau mirror and a 16th-century bottle that survived being unearthed by a digger.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Camera crews follow the activities of wildlife in the Arctic and Antarctic, including an emperor penguin colony's nine-month migration across sea ice to its breeding ground. The team also takes aerial and underwater shots to capture humpback whales feeding and tracks a male polar bear as it swims 100km to find new ice from which to hunt seals.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A delightful collection of British classics comes up for auction, including an Austin A35 and a Hillman Avenger Estate in stunning condition. An E-Type Jaguar also hits the auction.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Nazi Victory: The Post-War Plan
programme.desc: Guy Walters explores Hitler's plans for the future after the Second World War, beginning by revealing what Britain under Nazi occupation would have looked like.(n)

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring the motivations of key figures in Hitler's regime, beginning with propaganda master Josef Goebbels.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the nation with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins by travelling from High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, to Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire, finding out about the remarkable craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovering how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage and seeing Shakespeare through the eyes of a 19th-century rail tourist.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns how the railways helped to make Birmingham the pen-making capital of the world, hears the chilling tale of one of 19th-century Britain's most notorious murderers and samples delicacies concocted in a Victorian kitchen at Shugborough Hall, Staffordshire.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.desc: An exploration of the Harz Mountain Railway in Germany - Europe's longest rail network - which still uses steam locomotives and has a murky, hidden past.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Oz finds himself back in England after a night of heavy drinking - but has no idea how he got there. Tim Healy, Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet

programme.title: Open All Hours

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II. He sailed in from Holland around Lyme's famous Cobb with a handful of soldiers, believing the people would support him in overthrowing the newly crowned James II, who was also Monmouth's uncle, but, more importantly, Roman Catholic. The year was 1685 and the country was riven by anti-Catholic feeling mixed up with revolutionary zeal. Monmouth marched through the fiercely independent towns of Devon, gathering support from artisans and farmworkers. Meeting up with Billy Bragg in Colyton, Tony learns about the kind of people prepared to risk their lives for the Monmouth Rebellion.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A fuel plant in Poland built to make Nazi Germany self-sufficient by using the latest technology of the time to turn coal into oil.(n)

programme.title: Murder Maps
programme.desc: Nicholas Day recreates the case of a soldier who returned from the front line of the Second World War with a terrible urge to kill.(n)

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: Documentary series exploring the motivations of key figures in Hitler's regime, beginning with propaganda master Josef Goebbels.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two episodes from Balmoral, where she takes a tour of the grounds in a 1953 land rover and views archive footage of the royal family. Valuables brought in to be viewed include a collection of medals from both World Wars presented to a man who also rescued survivors from the Titanic, an art nouveau mirror and a 16th-century bottle that survived being unearthed by a digger.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A delightful collection of British classics comes up for auction, including an Austin A35 and a Hillman Avenger Estate in stunning condition. An E-Type Jaguar also hits the auction.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the nation with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins by travelling from High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, to Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire, finding out about the remarkable craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovering how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage and seeing Shakespeare through the eyes of a 19th-century rail tourist.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns how the railways helped to make Birmingham the pen-making capital of the world, hears the chilling tale of one of 19th-century Britain's most notorious murderers and samples delicacies concocted in a Victorian kitchen at Shugborough Hall, Staffordshire.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents a Christmas special from Lyme Park in Cheshire, where she and toy expert Will Farmer look back at children's Christmas presents dating back to Edwardian times. In the house's gardens, over 2000 visitors have arrived to have their items valued, including many with a royal heritage. The programme features a cup and saucer once owned by the Queen, a pram from Buckingham Palace, and a dagger believed to have been owned by Charles I. Plus, a celebration of the works of Charles Dickens.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The wildlife on the great plains of Africa, Asia, North America and the Arctic, which cover a quarter of the land on Earth. The distinctive Tibetan fox is captured on film for the first time, while a herd of caribou migrates 2,000 miles across the tundra in search of grass. Night vision equipment tracks a pride of 30 lions hunting elephants in the tropical African savannah near one of the region's few remaining waterholes. Presented by David Attenborough.(n)

programme.desc: Martin and Frasier vie for the affections of the boys' former babysitter, and the shrink becomes depressed when his father wins her love. Luckily, his ability to solve several mind-twisting problems puts him in a better mood. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and John Mahoney.(n)

programme.title: Frasier

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Harriet races against time to find a partially blind bull elephant that is seriously injured, while Emily and Annie try to track down a roan antelope that has bolted from its herd.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Home: Building for a Billionaire
programme.desc: Following the complex build of an extraordinary home costing millions in Mayfair, London, which belongs to self-made billionaire and philanthropist John Caudwell. Having sold Phones 4u in 2006 for just under 1.5 billion pounds, John is now aiming to create one of the most lavish and expensive homes ever to be renovated in Britain. As the build enters the final crucial stages, the programme follows this unique and challenging engineering project as a team of expert trades people and interior designers attempt to pull off one of the most extraordinary renovations in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of engineer Richard Bennett and his partner Felicia Böhm as they transform an underground water reservoir near the Humber Estuary into a family home. In 2013, the pair decided to embark on the ambitious build with a budget of only £150,000, but it proved a monumental challenge, and design and layout decisions stretched their abilities and the schedule to the max.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: From entertainment juggernauts to the week's biggest news stories and from hard-hitting documentary series to gritty drama, a cast of well-known faces offers sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques of popular and topical TV shows, sparking debate and giving viewers the chance to share those 'we said that' moments. This episode features Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Joe Swash and Stacey Solomon, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Nicola Adams and her partner Ella, and Harry Redknapp and his wife Sandra.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: A look at how sole eyewitness Joanne Lees came under immense scrutiny and pressure from both the British and Australian press as they hounded her continuously for an interview, while certain detectives were also sceptical of her version of events. The programme includes detailed sections of a video showing Joanne being confronted by detectives about seeming inconsistences in her account of the attack.(n)

programme.title: The Fantastical Factory of Curious Craft
programme.desc: Full-time carer Katie, teacher Rochelle, hairdresser Hayley and drag artist Miss Penny Press must create a superhero costume from scratch in just four hours, in a bid to impress Keith Lemon and be picked to go through to create a one-off craft for this week's celebrity clients, socialite couple Spencer Matthews and Vogue Williams. Keith chooses his favourite three to go on to craft a bespoke piece for Spencer and Vogue, who are after a unique Scottish-themed centre piece for Vogue's birthday party at Spencer's family home in the Scottish Highlands.(n)

programme.title: Grayson's Art Club
programme.desc: The theme this week is Britain and what that means to viewers and artists, with Grayson chatting to actress and broadcaster Liza Tarbuck about her own creation. Artists the Singh Twins discuss their lockdown art and photographer Martin Parr shares his isolation work.(n)

programme.title: Dispatches
programme.sub-title: Britain's Coronavirus Catastrophe: Did the Government Get it Wrong?: Dispatches
programme.desc: Examining the the British government's approach to dealing with the coronavirus pandemic. With Britain's confirmed death rate from coronavirus now one of the highest in the world, and the Government's strategy to get the economy restarted and prevent a second spike still developing, Dispatches reporter Antony Barnett begins asking questions that may shed a light on how to beat the virus in the future. With the input of people at the heart of events, Barnett investigates the eight astonishing weeks that saw the UK move into lockdown, and reveals the key decisions and moments that led to the UK shutting down and that have determined what has happened since.(n)

Will a bit of witty repartee and the revelation about his own furry friend encourage her to let her guard down? programme.title: Women Who Kill
programme.desc: With more than 30,000 women in prisons in America serving lengthy sentences for murder, this documentary looks at some of the extraordinary stories of women who committed these crimes, and the reasons why they killed people they knew and loved. They include Amber Hilberling, who was 19 and pregnant when she pushed her husband out of a window, Patricia Ignacio, who admitted to bludgeoning her cousin to death with a rock, and Ana Trujillo, who murdered her boyfriend by stabbing him with a stiletto shoe.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. The concluding episode examines how DNA found on Joanne Lees' shirt and the couple's Kombi van were decisive in the murder trial of Bradley Murdoch, but even with this evidence, there are serious questions still to be answered in this controversial case.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

Nine years on, this documentary examines her kidnapping and why people close to the case believe the police should reopen their investigation. However, the neighbours have got a court order to prevent him setting up the company. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis try to solve the murder of a college student whose death was depicted in graphic detail on the internet. However, the investigation grinds to a halt when they encounter a lawyer who is unwilling to reveal vital information about her client's past.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer is kidnapped, but manages to escape her captor. The NCIS team members investigate and soon discover a possible connection to her missing husband. Meanwhile, Abby knits both Quinn and Torres personalised 'gifts'.(n)

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: A passenger at Bristol Airport is caught smuggling cigarettes and admits to doing it for more than 10 years, and a suspicious bag is seized at Gatwick with £55,000 of cannabis inside. Plus, the crew of the fast cutter searcher investigates mysterious pot-markers off the Cornish coast.(n)

programme.title: Customs UK
programme.desc: Customs officers at Bristol catch three passengers carrying 87kg of tobacco. Claiming the amount is for personal use they put up a fight when they learn they may not be allowed to keep it. The crew of the Searcher boards a yacht on the trail of drugs, and a sniffer dog picks up a scent at Gatwick that turns out to be cocaine. John Rand narrates.(n)

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: Troi is forced to take over as captain after Picard becomes trapped in a lift following a collision between the Enterprise and a quantum filament. programme.sub-title: He lokomaika'i ka manu o K Aiona (Kind Is the Bird of Kaiona)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.sub-title: The Tears of Uther Pendragon - Part One
programme.desc: A year after her disappearance, Morgana returns to Camelot, where a delighted Uther immediately announces a banquet in her honour. However, when evil magic begins to take hold of the king's mind, Merlin suspects the lady is hiding a sinister secret, only for the wizard's search for the truth to leave him at the mercy of the scorpion-like enchanted serkets. Colin Morgan and Katie McGrath star.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: Warrick testifies at a rape and murder hearing and discovers the weapon used to kill the victim cannot be submitted as evidence because it was seized during an unlawful search. programme.desc: Part two of two. programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over a year following life at the House of Lords, one of Britain's oldest, most idiosyncratic and most important institutions, going behind the scenes during a turbulent 12 months that has seen dramatic changes in the political landscape and the Lords battling it out with the Government. The first edition introduces the larger-than-life characters that populate the House, including new people's peers, hereditaries and political grandees, as they try to change legislation and follow their personal passions.(n)

programme.title: Man Friday (1975)
programme.desc: Shipwrecked mariner Robinson Crusoe is overjoyed to discover he has a fellow castaway, a black islander whom he names Friday, and insists in treating as a servant and trying to convert to Western culture. Friday quickly grasps that Crusoe will never see him as an equal, and uses his cunning to turns the tables on the Englishman. Comedy, starring Peter O'Toole and Richard Roundtree.(n)

programme.title: Portrait from Life (1949)
programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling.(n)

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home.(n)

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives
programme.desc: Behind the scenes on the record-breaking 40,000 nautical mile race around the world on 70-foot ocean racing yachts. Follow the inside story of what they go through to take part.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Plainclothes police on patrol run into an argument outside a shop, but when they try and find out what is going on, there is a surprise none of them expected.(n)

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War.(n)

programme.title: UCMMA
programme.desc: Dave O'Donnell, CJ and Grant Waterman present MMA action from Troxy London.(n)

programme.title: Silk
programme.desc: Martha again represents Mark Draper, this time defending him at a murder trial, in which he is accused of stabbing a high court judge. However, the case becomes more complex when Mark's girlfriend Emma Slater is also implicated in the crime. Meanwhile, a chambers vote decides Nick and Niamh's fate, and Billy resorts to underhand tactics to make sure Shoe Lane receives more business from Cant Froggett Solicitors.(n)

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson.(n)

programme.title: Blood Feud (2016)
programme.desc: An ex-convict is released from 10 years in prison for killing a gangster's son - unaware that his best friend has been hired by his victim's father to murder him. When the truth emerges, the two old partners in crime decide to work together to make amends for their past misdeeds - even if it means crossing the vengeful crime boss. Crime drama, starring Paul King and Micky Curate.(n)

programme.title: Bread (1971)
programme.desc: A group of friends camps out on what turns out to be private property, and gets coerced into redecorating the owner's house in return for being allowed to stay. The squatters plan to stage a music festival without the landowner finding out - and shoot a porn movie to raise the money. Drama, starring Anthony Nigel and Peter Marinker.(n)
